frontier_justification: LLM agent frameworks are evolving rapidly in 2025-2026 toward production-grade, multi-agent, long-horizon systems. SWE-agent, OpenDevin, and Claude Computer Use define the current frontier of autonomous software engineering and computer use.

papers.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
papers.key_contribution: ReAct: interleaves reasoning traces and tool-use actions in the LLM context; Thought→Action→Observation loop enables complex multi-step task completion.

idea_genome.problem_genome: LLMs can either reason (CoT) or act (tool use) but not synergistically integrate both in a principled loop.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: ReAct prompt: chain Thought (reasoning), Action (tool call with args), Observation (result) in the context; LLM generates next thought based on observations.
idea_genome.observation_genome: ReAct outperforms chain-of-thought alone on HotpotQA (+6%), Fever (+4%), and interactive task completion; interpretable reasoning traces.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: No memory across episodes; single-agent; cannot learn from feedback; error propagation in long action sequences; no structured reflection.

papers.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
papers.key_contribution: Reflexion: agent verbally reflects on task failures and stores reflections in episodic memory; enables improvement over multiple trials without gradient updates.

idea_genome.problem_genome: ReAct agents repeat same errors across episodes; no mechanism to learn from failure without expensive model fine-tuning.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: After each episode: evaluator judges success/failure; reflector generates verbal analysis of errors; store in episodic memory; next episode uses memory in prompt.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Reflexion achieves 91% on HumanEval coding (vs 80% CoT); significantly improves over multiple trials on sequential decision-making tasks.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Memory grows unboundedly; reflection quality depends on evaluator accuracy; single-agent; no collaboration; memory not persistent across sessions.

papers.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
papers.key_contribution: SWE-agent: designs Agent-Computer Interface (ACI) with specialized file editing, bash commands, and context management; resolves 12.5% of SWE-bench issues.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Reflexion-based agents lack proper interfaces for software engineering: need file navigation, code editing, test execution at repository scale.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: ACI: specialized commands (open, scroll, search_file, edit); command history in context; linting feedback on edits; bash subprocess integration.
idea_genome.observation_genome: SWE-agent resolves 12.47% of SWE-bench (real GitHub issues) with Claude 3.5 Sonnet; best single-agent on benchmark at release.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Single-agent struggles on large codebases; no parallel execution; no long-term memory; requires complete task specification in context.

papers.title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Generation LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation
papers.key_contribution: AutoGen: multi-agent conversation framework where specialized agents (assistant, code executor, critic) communicate via structured conversation to solve complex tasks.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Single-agent systems (SWE-agent) bottleneck on complex tasks requiring planning, execution, and verification simultaneously.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Agents with defined roles (AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, GroupChatManager) communicate through a shared conversation buffer; code execution in sandbox.
idea_genome.observation_genome: AutoGen enables multi-agent workflows for math, coding, data analysis; demonstrably solves tasks that single agents cannot; widely adopted (>100K stars).
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Inter-agent communication overhead; agents can get stuck in loops; no principled role assignment; scalability to many agents unclear.

papers.title: OpenDevin: An Open Platform for AI Software Developers as Generalist Agents
papers.key_contribution: OpenDevin (now OpenHands): open-source platform unifying SWE-agent's ACI with AutoGen's multi-agent framework; sandbox environment with browser + bash + file system.

idea_genome.problem_genome: SWE-agent (single-agent) and AutoGen (multi-agent, no ACI) are separate; need unified open platform combining ACI, multi-agent, and diverse computer use.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Event-driven multi-agent architecture; sandbox with Docker isolation; CodeAct framework: code as actions; delegates to specialist sub-agents.
idea_genome.observation_genome: OpenDevin achieves 26.7% on SWE-bench with CodeAct; 28.8% on SWE-bench verified; broad task coverage including web browsing and file editing.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Docker overhead; still fails on >50% of SWE-bench issues; long-horizon planning remains brittle; communication protocol between agents not standardized.

papers.title: SWE-World: Building Software Engineering Agents in Docker-Free Environments
papers.key_contribution: Replaces physical Docker execution sandbox with LLM surrogate that simulates test feedback; enables Docker-free SFT and RL training for SE agents; raises Qwen2.5-Coder-32B on SWE-bench Verified from 6.2% to 52.0% via SFT, 55.0% with RL, and 68.2% with test-time scaling.

idea_genome.problem_genome: OpenDevin requires Docker for physical code execution; Docker overhead limits training throughput; scaling RL training for SE agents requires millions of execution calls infeasible at physical execution speed.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: LLM-based surrogate environment trained on actual agent-interaction data to predict execution outcomes and test results; enables agent training without costly Docker setup; test-time scaling via simulated evaluation of candidate solutions.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Docker-free SFT achieves 52.0%, Docker-free RL achieves 55.0%, and TTS achieves 68.2% on SWE-bench Verified — a 62-point gain from the 6.2% baseline.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Simulated environment diverges from real execution for complex multi-file refactors; agent-environment gap grows with task complexity; no mechanism for the agent and environment model to co-evolve.

edges.evolution_focus: Reflexion adds verbal reinforcement and episodic memory to ReAct's action loop; same Thought-Action-Observation structure, new self-improvement mechanism.
Reflexion's reflection mechanism applied to software engineering domain with specialized ACI; adapts the agent feedback loop to repository-scale coding tasks.
AutoGen shifts from single-agent with specialized interface to multi-agent conversation paradigm; role-based agent specialization is a fundamentally different architectural approach.
OpenDevin hybridizes SWE-agent's specialized ACI (single-agent) with AutoGen's multi-agent conversation framework into a unified open-source platform.

SWE-World replaces physical Docker execution environment with a learned world model surrogate; paradigm shift from agents operating in real execution environments to agents trained and tested in simulated world models.

open_question: SWE-World achieves 68.2% on SWE-bench via learned environment simulation, but the simulated environment diverges from real execution for complex multi-file refactors. What mechanism would enable a continuously self-improving agent-environment co-evolution — where the agent's failures update the environment model and the improved environment produces better agent training signal?

question.name: Question
question.description: Present only the domain/subfield and the curated May-2026 research question; ask the model to propose a next-step research direction in LLM Agents / Agentic AI.

library.name: Library
library.description: Present the curated research frontier plus 6 trace papers in random order with paper titles and brief abstracts, but without explicit lineage order, genome fields, or dynamics labels; ask the model to propose the next research direction.

lineage.name: Lineage
lineage.description: Present the curated research frontier plus the ordered trace with genome metadata, GenomeDiff-style change evidence, and parent-child relationships; ask the model to predict the next evolutionary step without naming predefined category labels.

frontier_evidence.title: SWE-World: Building Software Engineering Agents in Docker-Free Environments
frontier_evidence.unresolved_gap: Simulated environment diverges from real execution for complex multi-file refactors; agent-environment gap grows with task complexity; no mechanism for the agent and environment model to co-evolve.
frontier_evidence.why_frontier: Used as the May 2026 frontier anchor: the arena question targets a limitation that remains unresolved at the endpoint of this lineage.

frontier_status.manual_review: Re-audited on 2026-05-05 for appendix release: active arena trace has a 2025/2026 frontier endpoint, a non-solved open research question, valid forward lineage edges, and generation settings aligned with post-hoc dynamics evaluation.
frontier_status.prompt_role: The same research question is shown in Question, Library, and Lineage settings; only the supporting literature/lineage context changes.
